verses.text: The LORD: the appointed feasts of the LORD that ye shall proclaim are holy convocations. Six days shall work be done; the seventh day is a sabbath of rest, a holy convocation. Do no work — it is the sabbath of the LORD in all your dwellings. Guidance: The list of feasts begins with the weekly one. Sabbath is the smallest and most repeated holy day — fifty-two times a year. The annual feasts grow out of the weekly rest.

The LORD: the appointed feasts of the LORD that ye shall proclaim are holy convocations. Six days shall work be done; the seventh day is a sabbath of rest, a holy convocation. Do no work — it is the sabbath of the LORD in all your dwellings. Guidance: The list of feasts begins with the weekly one. Sabbath is the smallest and most repeated holy day — fifty-two times a year. The annual feasts grow out of the weekly rest.

The LORD: the appointed feasts of the LORD that ye shall proclaim are holy convocations. Six days shall work be done; the seventh day is a sabbath of rest, a holy convocation. Do no work — it is the sabbath of the LORD in all your dwellings. Guidance: The list of feasts begins with the weekly one. Sabbath is the smallest and most repeated holy day — fifty-two times a year. The annual feasts grow out of the weekly rest.

Passover — first month, fourteenth day at even. Feast of Unleavened Bread — fifteenth day, seven days; first day holy convocation, no servile work; offer fire offering seven days; seventh day holy convocation. Guidance: The year begins with deliverance. Passover for one night, then seven days without leaven — Israel relives the night of the exodus every spring. The Last Supper sits inside this very feast; Christ becomes its meaning.

Firstfruits — when ye come into the land and reap, bring a sheaf to the priest; he waves it the morrow after the sabbath. Offer a lamb for burnt offering, meat with oil, drink of wine. Eat no bread or grain till the offering — statute forever. Guidance: The first heads of grain go up before any loaf is baked. Paul calls Christ the firstfruits of those who sleep — He rose on the morrow after the sabbath, the very day of this feast.

Number fifty days from the morrow after the sabbath — bring two wave loaves with leaven. With the bread: seven lambs, a bullock, two rams for burnt; a kid for sin; two lambs for peace. Holy convocation, no work. When ye reap, leave the corners. Guidance: Pentecost is fifty days after the sheaf — wheat is now flour, flour is now bread. The Spirit fell on the church on this exact day. Acts 2 is woven into Leviticus 23.

Trumpets — seventh month, first day; a sabbath of rest, memorial of blowing trumpets, a holy convocation. Do no servile work; offer a fire offering to the LORD. Guidance: A new year, a new month, trumpets shouting. The shofar gathers Israel before the heaviest day of the year. The trumpet is also the sound of Christ's return; Pentecost-shofar to last-trumpet, the same call.

Day of Atonement — tenth day of the seventh month; holy convocation, afflict your souls, fire offering. Do no work — atone before the LORD. Whoever not afflicted — cut off. Whoever works — destroyed. Sabbath of rest from the ninth day at even. Guidance: Once a year Israel fasts. Affliction of soul before God is not optional; it is the day. No work, no food, only sorrow and blood. Yet from that bare day comes the freshness of forgiveness.

Tabernacles — fifteenth day of the seventh month, seven days. First day holy convocation; eighth day solemn assembly. Take boughs, palms, willows; rejoice. Dwell in booths — that your generations may know I made Israel dwell in booths when I brought them out of Egypt: I am the LORD your God. Guidance: A week of camping out, on purpose. Israel remembers the wilderness by living in it again — palm-leaf shelters, open sky. Joy and memory braided. God's people learn never to forget the way home.
